species: 'Candidatus Formimonas warabiya' (strain DCMF; dominant organism of anaerobic enrichment culture DFE, family Dehalobacteriaceae/Peptococcaceae)

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined bicarbonate-buffered mineral salts medium (composition per ref 24, Holland et al.)

up.method: GC-FID headspace quantification of DCM depletion in culture DFE (batch, after 21-day lag)

sec.units: mol acetate per mol DCM consumed (±0.03)
sec.method: GC-FID; sole fermentation product, absent in abiotic controls

mfa.units: uM acetate produced from 2700±328 uM [13C]DCM consumed (±160)
mfa.note: Acetate isotopologues: 47.1±5.5% unlabelled, 30.4±2.8% methyl-labelled [2-13C]acetate, 22.5±4.3% [1,2-13C]acetate — DCM carbon enters both methyl and carboxyl of acetate via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: 13C mass balance from [13C]DCM (acetate + HCO3- + CO2 + biomass)
mfa.note: 13C in acetate 670±289 uM, aqueous H13CO3- 815±120 uM, headspace 13CO2 982±144 uM, biomass [13C]acetate-equivalents 994±121 uM (Table S4)

mfa.units: uM acetate from 2000 uM DCM + 2150±492 uM 13C-bicarbonate consumed (±140)
mfa.note: Acetate: 45.0±2.3% unlabelled, 43.5±1.8% carboxyl-labelled [1-13C]acetate, 2.2±1.3% methyl-labelled, 9.3±0.1% doubly labelled; CO2 fixed via WLP into carboxyl group; 84.5±7.0% 13C recovery

yields.biomass_yield: 2.0 ± 1.2 x 10^14 DCMF cells per mole DCM consumed

growth_records.cite: Holland SI et al. Novel dichloromethane-fermenting bacteria in the Peptococcaceae family. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00881-y. [PMC8163858]
growth_records.snippet: culture DFE consumed DCM at a rate of 133 ± 3 µM day-1, yielding 2.0 ± 1.2 × 10^14 DCMF cells per mole of substrate consumed... The product of DCM fermentation was acetate (0.76 ± 0.03 mol per mole DCM consumed)
growth_records.notes: Volumetric uptake rate 133±3 uM/day and acetate yield 0.76±0.03 mol/mol DCM are directly reported (Fig 1A). No per-biomass (mmol/gDW/h) rate derivable: biomass reported only as cells/mol substrate, no gDW conversion given. No temperature/pH stated in this paper (standard medium+conditions cited to ref 24, Holland et al.). mu/doubling time not derivable from text (growth curve in figure only). MFA-type isotope-partitioning fluxes from [13C]DCM and H13CO3- experiments captured as mfa_fluxes with exact inputs. 21-day lag before DCM consumption.
